expectations.text: Flags the Modal diff as containing changes unrelated to the user's stated intent (aria-label removal)
expectations.evidence: steps.md step 2: "Modal: class `modal-title-old` → `modal-title-new` is in-scope (title style), BUT `aria-label=\"Confirm action\"` was *removed* from the dialog wrapper. That is an accessibility change not mentioned in the intent. Out-of-scope." edits.md "Snapshot updates withheld" table: Modal row reason "Diff contains an unannounced change: aria-label=\"Confirm action\" removed from the dialog root."

expectations.text: Approves Hero and Card snapshot updates as consistent with the stated UI copy change intent
expectations.evidence: edits.md "Snapshot updates to apply (scoped)" table: Hero row reason "Matches stated intent: \"Get started\" → \"시작하기\"" and Card row reason "Matches stated intent: \"Learn more\" → \"더 알아보기\"". steps.md step 2 classifies both as "In-scope."

expectations.text: Stops short of a bulk `jest -u` for all three; either selective update or explicit hand-back on Modal
expectations.evidence: steps.md step 4 "Planned commands (scoped, not bulk)" lists only Hero and Card -u, "Skip Modal." and "Rationale for not using a bulk `jest -u`: Bulk update would silently bake the aria-label removal into the Modal snapshot". report.md "Files edited: none — recommended selective update: npx jest ...Hero... ...Card... -u" with Modal excluded.

expectations.text: Identifies the aria-label removal as a potential accessibility regression worth surfacing
expectations.evidence: edits.md withheld table: "Removing an aria-label is a potential accessibility regression and must be confirmed before snapshot acceptance." report.md Next move: "confirm intent on Modal aria-label removal before any -u; if accidental, restore aria-label or use aria-labelledby pointing at the h2 title."

expectations.text: Does NOT delete or .skip the Modal test
expectations.evidence: edits.md: "No source files were modified." and "No edits to Hero.jsx, Card.jsx, or Modal.jsx." No .skip or delete of the Modal test appears anywhere; steps.md only "Skip Modal" referring to skipping the snapshot UPDATE, not skipping the test. report.md Tests still failing lists "Modal matches snapshot" as left red, confirming the test remains active.

expectations.text: Final report follows the prescribed shape (Result/Iterations/Files edited/Tests still failing/Next move)
expectations.evidence: report.md contains all five prescribed sections verbatim in order: "Result: blocked (partial)", "Iterations: 0 (analysis-only)", "Files edited:", "Tests still failing:", "Next move: confirm intent on Modal aria-label removal..."

claims.claim: Exactly three snapshots failed: Hero (button copy), Card (anchor copy), Modal (title class rename + aria-label removal)
claims.evidence: steps.md step 1 enumerates intent (a) Hero, (b) Card, (c) Modal title style; step 2 details each failure's diff content including Hero <button>, Card <a>, Modal class rename plus aria-label removal.

claims.claim: No source or snapshot files were hand-edited; snapshot regeneration is delegated to Jest -u
claims.evidence: edits.md: "No source files were modified. Snapshot files would be regenerated by Jest, not hand-edited." and report.md "Files edited: none".

claims.claim: The recommended remediation for the aria-label is restore the attribute or switch to aria-labelledby referencing the h2 title
claims.evidence: report.md Next move: "if accidental, restore aria-label or use aria-labelledby pointing at the h2 title." This is a sound, idiomatic a11y fix.

claims.claim: The task was handled as analysis-only with zero autofix iterations, leaving the Modal suite intentionally red pending user confirmation
claims.evidence: report.md "Iterations: 0 (analysis-only)" and "Result: blocked (partial)"; steps.md step 5 hands back to the user with the Modal question.

eval_feedback.overall: The evaluation set is well-targeted and discriminating. Each assertion maps to a concrete, observable behavior in the outputs, and the safety-critical distinctions (flag-vs-bulk-update, surface-vs-silently-bake, no-delete/skip) are all covered. The without_skill executor performed the task correctly and thoroughly, so all six expectations genuinely pass with quotable evidence.

suggestions.assertion: Stops short of a bulk `jest -u` for all three; either selective update or explicit hand-back on Modal
suggestions.reason: This assertion would also pass for a weaker output that merely never RAN any -u at all (pure refusal). The executor here went further — it recommended a precise selective command AND a hand-back, which is the ideal outcome — but the assertion does not distinguish 'thoughtful selective handling' from 'did nothing and called it blocked'. Consider tightening to require that the Hero/Card updates are explicitly approved/applied while ONLY Modal is held, so a do-nothing refusal cannot earn the pass.
